level_signals: 3–4+ years of experience in Revenue Operations, GTM Engineering, Sales Operations, or a related role
Hands-on executor role, not a leadership or management position

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 3–4+ years of experience in Revenue Operations, GTM Engineering, Sales Operations, or a related role
Strong hands-on Salesforce experience (admin, reporting, flows, automations)
Incredible AI curiosity and capability, using tools like Cursor and Claude to optimize workflows and improve processes.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience at a hyper-growth SaaS or AI company.
Experience with CPQ (Salesforce CPQ or similar quoting tools).
Familiarity with contract generation tools (e.g.,DocuSign Gen).
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with high attention to detail.
Comfort working in ambiguity at a fast-growing startup.
Strong cross-functional communication and stakeholder management skills.

scope.surface: RevOps team supporting go-to-market functions including quoting, billing, usage tracking, Salesforce administration, and reporting at a rapidly scaling AI company
scope.business_model: API-based SaaS / usage-based billing; B2B developer and enterprise customers
primary_metrics_inferred: RevOps request throughput and resolution time
core_responsibilities: Support Quote-to-Cash processes, including CPQ workflows, order form review, and contract provisioning
Support billing, invoicing, and monetization workflows in partnership with Finance
Reconcile customer usage, billing, and revenue data across CRM, product, and finance systems
Serve as a hands-on Salesforce administrator: build reports/dashboards, support flows and automations, maintain data quality
Triage, prioritize, and execute a wide range of RevOps requests
Proactively identify inefficiencies and help implement improvements
Leverage AI tools and automation to increase operational efficiency
Contribute to documentation and scalable RevOps processes

green_flags: AI-native culture with explicit tooling examples (Cursor, Claude) — practical rather than generic
Clearly scoped hands-on role with defined responsibilities
Series C-backed company with disclosed major investors and strong customer list
Benefits package is comprehensive including wellness, learning stipend, and 401(k) match
Equal opportunity employer statement with accommodation offer
red_flags: Location and remote/hybrid/onsite policy not stated anywhere in the JD
AI-first mindset framed as mandatory ('not optional') which may signal high pace and potential burnout risk
Many items in 'It Would Be Great If You Had' that are standard RevOps skills (analytical skills, stakeholder management) may be de facto required

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years requirement and role description; not stated)
location.type (not stated in JD; left as 'unclear')
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and customer examples)
compensation.equity_mentioned (no mention of equity anywhere in JD)
ambiguities: Several items in 'It Would Be Great If You Had' (analytical skills, stakeholder management, comfort with ambiguity) read as soft skills typically expected at this level; classified as nice_to_haves per JD section heading but may function as implicit requirements.
Location/work arrangement is completely absent from the JD — remote, hybrid, or onsite is unknown.
The note about Employer of Record for international employees implies global hiring is possible, but no specific geographies are mentioned for this role.
